summary: Only clear a target's resume cursor when the page loop exhausted the resource naturally — not when the --max-pages cap cut it short.
reason: Greptile finding on PR #433. After each per-target page loop, `syncResource` unconditionally wrote `SaveSyncState(ckey, "", totalCount)`, clobbering the resume cursor saved after the last fetched page. That is correct for natural exhaustion (the next run re-scans from the head) but wrong on a `--max-pages` cap hit: a workspace with more records than maxPages × pageLimit would re-fetch the same first maxPages pages on every run, leaving deeper records permanently unreachable. Track whether the loop broke on the cap (`capHit`) and skip the end-of-target cursor clear in that case so the next run resumes where it left off.
